what: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Analyse whether government’s debt sustainable path, using frameworks IMF European Commission use country assessments.

title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
code: install.packages("debtkit")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/debtkit") library(debtkit)  # Project debt forward 10 years proj <- dk_project(debt = 0.90, interest_rate = 0.04,                    gdp_growth = 0.03, primary_balance = 0.01, horizon = 10) proj #> -- Debt Sustainability Projection -- #> * Horizon: 10 years #> * Initial debt/GDP: 90% #> * Terminal debt/GDP: 82.6% #> * Debt-stabilising primary balance: 0.8%

title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Every government borrows money. central question public finance whether government can keep servicing debt, whether debt ratio spiral upward something breaks. Debt sustainability analysis (DSA) standard framework answering question. IMF, European Commission, finance ministries, central banks use . core idea simple. Next year’s debt--GDP ratio depends four things: year’s debt, interest rate debt, fast economy growing, whether government running surplus deficit (excluding interest payments). interest rates exceed GDP growth, debt snowballs. growth exceeds interest rates, debt stabilises easily. practice, full DSA combines several analyses: deterministic projections baseline assumptions, historical decompositions drove debt changes past, stress tests adverse scenarios, stochastic simulations generate thousands possible paths, tests whether government historically responds rising debt tightening policy, calculations much fiscal adjustment needed hit target. well-established methods, tedious code scratch. debtkit packages clean R functions plotting methods, can go raw fiscal data complete assessment lines code.

what: Where is the debt ratio heading?
title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Project debt forward constant assumptions. output tells terminal debt ratio primary balance stabilise debt current level.
code: library(debtkit)  proj <- dk_project(debt = 0.90, interest_rate = 0.04,                    gdp_growth = 0.03, primary_balance = 0.01, horizon = 10) proj #> -- Debt Sustainability Projection -- #> * Horizon: 10 years #> * Initial debt/GDP: 90% #> * Terminal debt/GDP: 82.6% #> * Debt-stabilising primary balance: 0.8%  plot(proj)

what: What drove debt changes in the past?
title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Decompose historical debt movements four components: interest costs pushing debt , GDP growth pulling , primary balance, residual (stock-flow adjustment).
code: d <- dk_sample_data() decomp <- dk_decompose(d$debt, d$interest_rate, d$gdp_growth,                         d$primary_balance, years = d$years) decomp #> -- Debt Decomposition -- #> * Periods: 19 (2005-2023) #> * Cumulative change: 24 pp #> *   Interest effect: 52.5 pp #> *   Growth effect: -58.2 pp #> *   Primary balance: 14.4 pp #> *   Stock-flow adj.: 15.3 pp  plot(decomp)  # Stacked bar chart of contributions

what: How wide is the range of possible outcomes?
title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Estimate joint distribution macro shocks historical data, simulate 1,000 debt paths. fan chart shows 10th 90th percentile bands.
code: d <- dk_sample_data() shocks <- dk_estimate_shocks(d$gdp_growth, d$interest_rate, d$primary_balance) fan <- dk_fan_chart(debt = 0.90, interest_rate = 0.035, gdp_growth = 0.03,                     primary_balance = -0.01, shocks = shocks,                     n_sim = 1000, horizon = 10, seed = 42) fan #> -- Stochastic Debt Fan Chart -- #> * Simulations: 1000 #> * Horizon: 10 years #> * Initial debt: 90% of GDP #> * Baseline terminal debt: 103.2% of GDP  plot(fan)  # Fan chart with percentile bands

title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Run six standardised IMF stress-test scenarios: growth shock, interest rate shock, exchange rate shock, primary balance shock, combined shock, contingent liabilities materialising.
code: stress <- dk_stress_test(debt = 0.90, interest_rate = 0.04,                          gdp_growth = 0.03, primary_balance = 0.01,                          fx_share = 0.20) stress #> -- IMF Stress Test Scenarios -- #> * Horizon: 5 years #> * Initial debt/GDP: 90% #> #> Terminal debt/GDP by scenario: #>   Baseline                 85.5% #>   Growth shock             87.3% #>   Interest rate shock      93.9% #>   Exchange rate shock      88.2% #>   Primary balance shock    87.3% #>   Combined shock           89.8% #>   Contingent liabilities   94.8%  plot(stress)

what: Does the government respond to rising debt?
title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Estimate Bohn’s (1998) fiscal reaction function. positive, statistically significant coefficient means government systematically raises primary surplus debt rises, satisfying sufficient condition sustainability.
code: d <- dk_sample_data() bohn <- dk_bohn_test(d$primary_balance, d$debt, robust_se = TRUE) bohn #> -- Bohn Fiscal Reaction Function -- #> * Method: ols (HAC) #> * Observations: 20 #> * rho = 0.15 (SE = 0.06, p = 0.02) #> v Sustainable: rho > 0 and significant at 5% level.

what: How much fiscal adjustment is needed?
title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Compute European Commission’s S1 S2 sustainability gap indicators. S1 measures adjustment needed reach 60% debt 20 years. S2 measures adjustment needed stabilise debt infinite horizon, accounting ageing costs.
code: dk_sustainability_gap(   debt = 0.90, structural_balance = -0.01,   gdp_growth = 0.015, interest_rate = 0.025, ageing_costs = 0.02 ) #> -- Sustainability Gap Indicators -- #> * Current debt/GDP: 90% #> * Current structural PB: -1% #> #> -- S1 Indicator -- #> * Required PB adjustment: 2.8 pp #> * Target debt/GDP: 60% in 20 years #> #> -- S2 Indicator -- #> * Required PB adjustment: 1.9 pp

title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Every function debtkit takes four inputs, decimals (0.90 = 90% GDP): standard fiscal variables published every major data provider. can type directly, use built-sample data, pull data source.

title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: know numbers (budget document, news article, textbook exercise), just type :
code: library(debtkit)  # Italy-like scenario: high debt, low growth, small deficit dk_project(debt = 1.40, interest_rate = 0.035, gdp_growth = 0.02,            primary_balance = 0.015, horizon = 10)

what: Option 2: Use the built-in sample data
title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: package includes sample datasets can try everything immediately:

what: Option 3: Pull data from a source
title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: complete example using OECD data 38 member countries:
code: # 1. Install the data package (one time) install.packages("readoecd")  # 2. Download fiscal data for France library(readoecd) debt <- oecd_series("GGDEBT", "FRA")          # General government debt (% of GDP) growth <- oecd_series("NGDP_RPCH", "FRA")      # Real GDP growth (%) balance <- oecd_series("GGXONLB_NGDP", "FRA")  # Primary balance (% of GDP)  # 3. Convert from percent to decimal and feed into debtkit library(debtkit) dk_project(   debt = tail(debt$value, 1) / 100,   interest_rate = 0.03,                         # Use latest effective rate estimate   gdp_growth = tail(growth$value, 1) / 100,   primary_balance = tail(balance$value, 1) / 100,   horizon = 10 )

title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: objects returned dk_project(), dk_decompose(), dk_fan_chart(), dk_stress_test(), dk_bohn_test(), dk_compare() print(), summary(), plot() methods.

title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment
text: Blanchard, O.J. (1990). “Suggestions New Set Fiscal Indicators.” OECD Economics Department Working Papers, . 79. doi:10.1787/435618162862 Bohn, H. (1998). “Behavior U.S. Public Debt Deficits.” Quarterly Journal Economics, 113(3), 949-963. doi:10.1162/003355398555793 Ghosh, .R., Kim, J.., Mendoza, E.G., Ostry, J.D. Qureshi, M.S. (2013). “Fiscal Fatigue, Fiscal Space Debt Sustainability Advanced Economies.” Economic Journal, 123(566), F4-F30. IMF (2013). Staff Guidance Note Public Debt Sustainability Analysis Market-Access Countries. IMF Policy Paper. IMF (2022). title: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment

what: debtkit: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment — debtkit-package
title: debtkit: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment — debtkit-package
text: Analyses government debt sustainability using standard debt dynamics framework Blanchard (1990) doi:10.1787/budget-v2-art12-en  IMF Debt Sustainability Analysis methodology (IMF, 2013) Sovereign Risk Debt Sustainability Framework (IMF, 2022). Projects debt--GDP paths, decomposes historical debt changes interest, growth, primary balance contributions, estimates fiscal reaction functions following Bohn (1998) doi:10.1162/003355398555793 . Produces stochastic fan charts via Monte Carlo simulation, standardised stress tests, IMF- style heat map risk assessments. Computes S1/S2 sustainability gap indicators used European Commission. methods pure computation external dependencies beyond base R; works fiscal data source.

title: debtkit: Debt Sustainability Analysis and Fiscal Risk Assessment — debtkit-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

what: Bohn's Fiscal Reaction Function Test — dk_bohn_test
title: Bohn's Fiscal Reaction Function Test — dk_bohn_test
text: Estimates fiscal reaction function following Bohn (1998): pb(t) = rho * d(t-1) + alpha * Z(t) + epsilon(t), pb primary balance--GDP ratio, d lagged debt--GDP, Z matrix control variables.

title: Bohn's Fiscal Reaction Function Test — dk_bohn_test
code: dk_bohn_test(   primary_balance,   debt,   controls = NULL,   method = c("ols", "rolling", "quadratic"),   window = NULL,   robust_se = FALSE )

title: Bohn's Fiscal Reaction Function Test — dk_bohn_test
text: primary_balance Numeric vector primary balance--GDP ratios. debt Numeric vector lagged debt--GDP ratios (length primary_balance). controls Optional data.frame control variables (number rows primary_balance). column enters regression separate regressor. method Character; "ols" (default) single OLS regression full sample, "rolling" rolling-window regressions, "quadratic" non-linear specification includes squared debt term detect fiscal fatigue (Ghosh et al. 2013). window Integer; rolling window size. Required method = "rolling", ignored otherwise. robust_se Logical; TRUE, compute Newey-West HAC standard errors using Bartlett kernel automatic bandwidth floor(4*(n/100)^(2/9)). corrects serial correlation fiscal data. Default FALSE.

title: Bohn's Fiscal Reaction Function Test — dk_bohn_test
text: S3 object class dk_bohn components: rho Estimated fiscal response coefficient (full sample last rolling window). rho_se Standard error rho. rho_pvalue p-value test H0: rho = 0. sustainable Logical; TRUE rho > 0 rho_pvalue < 0.05. model lm object full-sample (OLS/quadratic) last-window (rolling) regression. method method used ("ols", "rolling", "quadratic"). rho_ts data.frame columns index, rho, rho_lower, rho_upper method = "rolling"; NULL otherwise. robust_se Logical; whether HAC standard errors used. rho2 Coefficient debt squared (method = "quadratic"). rho2_se Standard error rho2 (quadratic ). rho2_pvalue p-value rho2 (quadratic ). turning_point Debt level fiscal response peaks, -rho/(2*rho2) (quadratic ).

title: Bohn's Fiscal Reaction Function Test — dk_bohn_test
text: positive statistically significant rho indicates government systematically raises primary surplus response rising debt, satisfying sufficient condition debt sustainability.

title: Bohn's Fiscal Reaction Function Test — dk_bohn_test
text: Bohn, H. (1998). "Behavior U.S. Public Debt Deficits." Quarterly Journal Economics, 113(3), 949–963. doi:10.1162/003355398555793 Ghosh, .R., Kim, J.., Mendoza, E.G., Ostry, J.D. Qureshi, M.S. (2013). "Fiscal Fatigue, Fiscal Space Debt Sustainability Advanced Economies." Economic Journal, 123(566), F4–F30.

title: Bohn's Fiscal Reaction Function Test — dk_bohn_test
code: # Simulate data with positive fiscal response set.seed(42) n <- 50 debt <- cumsum(rnorm(n, 0.01, 0.02)) + 0.6 pb <- 0.04 * debt + rnorm(n, 0, 0.005) result <- dk_bohn_test(pb, debt) print(result) #>  #> ── Bohn Fiscal Reaction Function ─────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Method: ols #> • Observations: 50 #> • rho = 0.0365 (SE = 0.0058, p = 8.47e-08) #> ✔ Sustainable: rho > 0 and significant at 5% level.

title: Compare Debt Projections — dk_compare
text: Produces side--side comparison multiple debt--GDP projections, aligning year computing terminal values.

title: Compare Debt Projections — dk_compare
code: dk_compare(..., metric = "debt")

title: Compare Debt Projections — dk_compare
text: ... Named dk_projection objects compare. Names used scenario labels. metric Character. metric compare. Currently "debt" supported. Default "debt".

title: Compare Debt Projections — dk_compare
text: S3 object class dk_comparison containing: paths data.frame year column one column per scenario, giving debt--GDP path. terminal Named numeric vector terminal debt--GDP ratios.

title: Compare Debt Projections — dk_compare
code: d <- dk_sample_data() base <- dk_project(tail(d$debt, 1), 0.03, 0.04, 0.01, horizon = 5) austerity <- dk_project(tail(d$debt, 1), 0.03, 0.04, 0.03, horizon = 5) stimulus <- dk_project(tail(d$debt, 1), 0.03, 0.05, -0.01, horizon = 5)  comp <- dk_compare(   Baseline = base,   Austerity = austerity,   Stimulus = stimulus ) comp #>  #> ── Debt Projection Comparison ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • 3 scenarios: "Baseline", "Austerity", and "Stimulus" #>  #> Terminal debt/GDP: #>   Baseline             60.8% #>   Austerity            51% #>   Stimulus             67.5% plot(comp)

title: Decompose Historical Debt Changes — dk_decompose
text: Breaks observed year--year changes debt--GDP ratio four components:

title: Decompose Historical Debt Changes — dk_decompose
code: dk_decompose(debt, interest_rate, gdp_growth, primary_balance, years = NULL)

title: Decompose Historical Debt Changes — dk_decompose
text: debt Numeric vector historical debt--GDP ratios. interest_rate Numeric vector effective interest rates government debt. Must length debt. gdp_growth Numeric vector nominal GDP growth rates. Must length debt. primary_balance Numeric vector primary balance--GDP ratios (positive = surplus). Must length debt. years Optional integer vector year labels. Must length debt. NULL (default), years numbered sequentially.

title: Decompose Historical Debt Changes — dk_decompose
text: S3 object class dk_decomposition containing: data data.frame columns year, debt, change, interest_effect, growth_effect, snowball_effect, primary_balance_effect, sfa. years year labels used.

title: Decompose Historical Debt Changes — dk_decompose
text: Interest effect: \(r_t / (1 + g_t) \cdot d_{t-1}\) Growth effect: \(-g_t / (1 + g_t) \cdot d_{t-1}\) Primary balance effect: \(-pb_t\) Stock-flow adjustment (residual): actual change minus sum three identified components. standard decomposition used IMF (2013) European Commission. SFA residual captures privatisation receipts, exchange-rate valuation changes, --line operations, measurement error.

title: Decompose Historical Debt Changes — dk_decompose
text: Blanchard, O.J. (1990). Suggestions New Set Fiscal Indicators. OECD Economics Department Working Papers, . 79. doi:10.1787/budget-v2-art12-en International Monetary Fund (2013). Staff Guidance Note Public Debt Sustainability Analysis Market-Access Countries. IMF Policy Paper.

title: Decompose Historical Debt Changes — dk_decompose
code: d <- dk_sample_data() dec <- dk_decompose(   debt = d$debt,   interest_rate = d$interest_rate,   gdp_growth = d$gdp_growth,   primary_balance = d$primary_balance,   years = d$years ) dec #>  #> ── Debt Decomposition ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Periods: 19 (2005–2023) #> • Cumulative change: 24 pp #> •  Interest effect: 29.8 pp #> •  Growth effect: -39.5 pp #> •  Primary balance: 20.9 pp #> •  Stock-flow adj.: 12.8 pp plot(dec)

what: Estimate Joint Distribution of Macro Shocks — dk_estimate_shocks
title: Estimate Joint Distribution of Macro Shocks — dk_estimate_shocks
text: Estimates joint distribution GDP growth, interest rate, primary balance shocks use stochastic debt sustainability analysis. Three estimation methods supported: VAR(1) model (default), residual bootstrap, simple multivariate normal fit.

title: Estimate Joint Distribution of Macro Shocks — dk_estimate_shocks
code: dk_estimate_shocks(   gdp_growth,   interest_rate,   primary_balance,   method = c("var", "bootstrap", "normal"),   years = NULL )

title: Estimate Joint Distribution of Macro Shocks — dk_estimate_shocks
text: gdp_growth Numeric vector historical real GDP growth rates. interest_rate Numeric vector historical nominal (real) interest rates. primary_balance Numeric vector historical primary balance--GDP ratios. method Character; one "var" (default), "bootstrap", "normal". years Optional numeric vector year labels (length data).

title: Estimate Joint Distribution of Macro Shocks — dk_estimate_shocks
text: S3 object class dk_shocks components: vcov 3x3 variance-covariance matrix rows/columns named growth, interest_rate, primary_balance. means Named numeric vector variable means. method estimation method used. residuals Matrix residuals ("var" "bootstrap") NULL ("normal"). var_coefficients VAR(1) coefficient matrix ("var") NULL. n_obs Number observations used.

title: Estimate Joint Distribution of Macro Shocks — dk_estimate_shocks
text: method = "var", VAR(1) estimated equation--equation via OLS lagged system. residual variance-covariance matrix captures joint shock distribution. method = "bootstrap", VAR(1) estimated residuals stored block resampling. method = "normal", sample means covariance raw series used directly.

title: Estimate Joint Distribution of Macro Shocks — dk_estimate_shocks
code: set.seed(1) n <- 30 g <- rnorm(n, 0.02, 0.015) r <- rnorm(n, 0.03, 0.01) pb <- rnorm(n, -0.02, 0.01) shocks <- dk_estimate_shocks(g, r, pb) print(shocks) #>  #> ── Macro Shock Estimates ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Method: var #> • Observations: 29 #> • Variable means: #>     growth = 2.1%, interest_rate = 3.1%, primary_balance = -1.9% #> • Shock std. deviations: #>     growth = 1.4 pp, interest_rate = 0.8 pp, primary_balance = 0.9 pp #> • Shock correlation matrix: #>                 growth interest_rate primary_balance #> growth           1.000         0.068           0.041 #> interest_rate    0.068         1.000           0.388 #> primary_balance  0.041         0.388           1.000

title: Stochastic Debt Fan Chart — dk_fan_chart
text: Projects debt--GDP paths via Monte Carlo simulation using standard debt dynamics equation. step, correlated shocks growth, interest rate, primary balance drawn multivariate normal distribution added baseline paths. result fan chart showing distribution projected debt paths.

title: Stochastic Debt Fan Chart — dk_fan_chart
code: dk_fan_chart(   debt,   interest_rate,   gdp_growth,   primary_balance,   shocks = NULL,   shock_vcov = NULL,   n_sim = 1000L,   horizon = 5L,   confidence = c(0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9),   seed = NULL )

title: Stochastic Debt Fan Chart — dk_fan_chart
text: debt Numeric scalar; initial debt--GDP ratio. interest_rate Numeric scalar vector length horizon; baseline interest rate path. gdp_growth Numeric scalar vector length horizon; baseline GDP growth path. primary_balance Numeric scalar vector length horizon; baseline primary balance--GDP path. shocks dk_shocks object (dk_estimate_shocks()) providing shock distribution, NULL. shock_vcov Optional 3x3 variance-covariance matrix (alternative shocks). Rows/columns ordered: growth, interest_rate, primary_balance. Ignored shocks provided. n_sim Integer; number Monte Carlo simulations (default 1000). horizon Integer; projection horizon years (default 5). confidence Numeric vector quantile levels fan bands (default c(0.10, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90)). seed Optional integer seed reproducibility.

title: Stochastic Debt Fan Chart — dk_fan_chart
text: S3 object class dk_fan components: simulations Matrix dimension n_sim x (horizon + 1) containing simulated debt paths. quantiles Matrix quantiles time step, rows corresponding confidence levels. baseline Numeric vector length horizon + 1; deterministic baseline debt path. confidence quantile levels used. horizon projection horizon. prob_above Named list probability debt exceeding 60 percent, 90 percent, 120 percent GDP terminal year.

title: Stochastic Debt Fan Chart — dk_fan_chart
code: set.seed(1) n <- 30 g <- rnorm(n, 0.02, 0.015) r <- rnorm(n, 0.03, 0.01) pb <- rnorm(n, -0.02, 0.01) shocks <- dk_estimate_shocks(g, r, pb)  fan <- dk_fan_chart(   debt = 0.90,   interest_rate = 0.03,   gdp_growth = 0.02,   primary_balance = -0.02,   shocks = shocks,   n_sim = 500,   horizon = 10,   seed = 42 ) print(fan) #>  #> ── Stochastic Debt Fan Chart ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Simulations: 500 #> • Horizon: 10 years #> • Initial debt: 90% of GDP #> • Baseline terminal debt: 120.1% of GDP #> • Median terminal debt: 120.3% of GDP #> • Probability debt exceeds thresholds at horizon: #>     60% of GDP: 100% #>     90% of GDP: 100% #>     120% of GDP: 51.6%

title: Gross Financing Needs — dk_gfn
text: Computes gross financing needs (GFN) share GDP projection horizon. GFN represents total amount new borrowing government requires year cover primary deficit, interest payments, maturing debt:

title: Gross Financing Needs — dk_gfn
code: dk_gfn(debt, interest_rate, maturity_profile, primary_balance, horizon = 5)

title: Gross Financing Needs — dk_gfn
text: debt Numeric scalar. Initial debt--GDP ratio. interest_rate Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Effective nominal interest rate government debt. maturity_profile Numeric vector scalar. vector, gives share GDP maturing year horizon. scalar, interpreted average maturity years; debt assumed mature uniformly debt / maturity_profile per year. primary_balance Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Primary balance share GDP (positive = surplus). horizon Integer scalar. Projection horizon years. Default 5.

title: Gross Financing Needs — dk_gfn
text: data.frame columns: year Year index (1 horizon). primary_deficit Primary deficit (negative primary balance). interest_payments Interest payments share GDP. maturing_debt Maturing debt share GDP. gfn Total gross financing needs share GDP.

title: Gross Financing Needs — dk_gfn
text: $$GFN_t = -pb_t + r_t \cdot d_t + m_t$$ \(pb\) primary balance (positive = surplus), \(r\) effective interest rate, \(d\) debt--GDP, \(m\) maturing debt share GDP.

title: Gross Financing Needs — dk_gfn
text: International Monetary Fund (2013). Staff Guidance Note Public Debt Sustainability Analysis Market-Access Countries. IMF Policy Paper.

title: IMF-Style Risk Heat Map — dk_heat_map
text: Classifies sovereign debt risk low, medium, high based IMF (2013) thresholds debt--GDP, gross financing needs, optional debt-profile indicators. Advanced economies emerging markets use different thresholds.

title: IMF-Style Risk Heat Map — dk_heat_map
code: dk_heat_map(   debt,   gross_financing_needs,   debt_profile = NULL,   country_type = c("ae", "em") )

title: IMF-Style Risk Heat Map — dk_heat_map
text: debt Numeric scalar. Debt--GDP ratio. gross_financing_needs Numeric scalar. Gross financing needs share GDP. debt_profile Optional named list debt-profile indicators (ratios): share_st_debt Share short-term debt total debt. fx_share Share foreign-currency-denominated debt. nonresident_share Share debt held non-residents. bank_share Share debt held domestic banks. change_st_debt Year--year change share short-term debt (percentage points GDP). country_type Character. Either "ae" (advanced economy, default) "em" (emerging market).

title: IMF-Style Risk Heat Map — dk_heat_map
text: S3 object class dk_heatmap containing: ratings Named list risk ratings ("low", "medium", "high") indicator. overall Character. Overall risk level: "high" indicator high, "medium" medium, otherwise "low". values Named list input values. thresholds thresholds used classification. country_type country type used.

title: IMF-Style Risk Heat Map — dk_heat_map
text: International Monetary Fund (2013). Staff Guidance Note Public Debt Sustainability Analysis Market-Access Countries. IMF Policy Paper. International Monetary Fund (2022). Staff Guidance Note Sovereign Risk Debt Sustainability Framework Market Access Countries. IMF Policy Paper.

title: IMF-Style Risk Heat Map — dk_heat_map
code: hm <- dk_heat_map(   debt = 0.90,   gross_financing_needs = 0.18,   debt_profile = list(fx_share = 0.30, share_st_debt = 0.15),   country_type = "ae" ) hm #>  #> ── IMF Risk Heat Map (Advanced Economy) ──────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>   Debt / GDP                          90%    HIGH #>   Gross financing needs / GDP         18%    MEDIUM #>   Short-term debt share               15%    HIGH #>   Foreign currency share              30%    HIGH #>  #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>   Overall risk:                             HIGH

title: Project Debt-to-GDP Path — dk_project
text: Projects debt--GDP ratio forward using standard debt dynamics equation:

title: Project Debt-to-GDP Path — dk_project
code: dk_project(   debt,   interest_rate,   gdp_growth,   primary_balance,   sfa = 0,   horizon = 10,   date = NULL )

title: Project Debt-to-GDP Path — dk_project
text: debt Numeric scalar. Initial debt--GDP ratio (e.g., 0.90 90 per cent GDP). interest_rate Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Nominal effective interest rate government debt. gdp_growth Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Nominal GDP growth rate. primary_balance Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Primary balance share GDP. Positive values denote surplus; negative values deficit. sfa Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Stock-flow adjustment share GDP. Default 0. horizon Integer scalar. Number years project forward. Default 10. date Optional Date. supplied, projection anchored date (stored output labelling purposes).

title: Project Debt-to-GDP Path — dk_project
text: S3 object class dk_projection containing: debt_path Numeric vector length horizon + 1, giving debt--GDP ratio initial period terminal period. decomposition data.frame columns year, debt, interest_effect, growth_effect, snowball_effect, primary_balance_effect, sfa_effect, change. horizon projection horizon. inputs list storing input parameters.

title: Project Debt-to-GDP Path — dk_project
text: $$d_{t+1} = \frac{1 + r_t}{1 + g_t} d_t - pb_t + sfa_t$$ \(d\) debt--GDP ratio, \(r\) effective nominal interest rate government debt, \(g\) nominal GDP growth, \(pb\) primary balance share GDP (positive = surplus), \(sfa\) captures stock-flow adjustments (e.g. privatisation receipts, exchange-rate valuation changes, --line operations).

title: Project Debt-to-GDP Path — dk_project
text: Blanchard, O.J. (1990). Suggestions New Set Fiscal Indicators. OECD Economics Department Working Papers, . 79. doi:10.1787/budget-v2-art12-en International Monetary Fund (2013). Staff Guidance Note Public Debt Sustainability Analysis Market-Access Countries. IMF Policy Paper.

title: Project Debt-to-GDP Path — dk_project
code: d <- dk_sample_data() proj <- dk_project(   debt = tail(d$debt, 1),   interest_rate = 0.03,   gdp_growth = 0.04,   primary_balance = 0.01 ) proj #>  #> ── Debt Sustainability Projection ────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Horizon: 10 years #> • Initial debt/GDP: 69% #> • Terminal debt/GDP: 53.1% #> • Change: -15.9 pp #> • Debt-stabilising primary balance: -0.5% plot(proj)

what: Interest Rate-Growth Differential and Debt-Stabilising Primary Balance — dk_rg
title: Interest Rate-Growth Differential and Debt-Stabilising Primary Balance — dk_rg
text: Computes interest rate-growth differential (\(r - g\)), key indicator debt sustainability. \(r > g\), debt grows faster economy ("snowball effect" adverse) primary surplus needed stabilise debt ratio. \(r < g\), government can run primary deficit still see debt ratio fall.

title: Interest Rate-Growth Differential and Debt-Stabilising Primary Balance — dk_rg
code: dk_rg(interest_rate, gdp_growth, inflation = NULL, debt = NULL)

title: Interest Rate-Growth Differential and Debt-Stabilising Primary Balance — dk_rg
text: interest_rate Numeric. Effective nominal interest rate government debt. Scalar vector. gdp_growth Numeric. Nominal GDP growth rate. Scalar vector (length interest_rate). inflation Numeric NULL. supplied, inflation rate used compute real \(r - g\). Scalar length interest_rate. Default NULL. debt Numeric NULL. supplied, debt--GDP ratio used compute debt-stabilising primary balance. Scalar length interest_rate. Default NULL.

title: Interest Rate-Growth Differential and Debt-Stabilising Primary Balance — dk_rg
text: named list : rg_differential Numeric vector. nominal \(r - g\) differential. real_rg Numeric vector. real \(r - g\) differential. present inflation supplied. debt_stabilising_pb Numeric vector. debt-stabilising primary balance share GDP. present debt supplied.

title: Interest Rate-Growth Differential and Debt-Stabilising Primary Balance — dk_rg
text: debt supplied, function also computes debt-stabilising primary balance: primary surplus (share GDP) required hold debt--GDP ratio constant current level. given : $$pb^* = \frac{r - g}{1 + g} \cdot d$$ inflation supplied, function computes real \(r - g\) differential deflating interest rate GDP growth: \(r_{real} = (1 + r)/(1 + \pi) - 1\) \(g_{real} = (1 + g)/(1 + \pi) - 1\).

title: Interest Rate-Growth Differential and Debt-Stabilising Primary Balance — dk_rg
text: Blanchard, O.J. (1990). Suggestions New Set Fiscal Indicators. OECD Economics Department Working Papers, . 79. doi:10.1787/budget-v2-art12-en Barrett, P. (2018). Interest-Growth Differentials Debt Limits Advanced Economies. IMF Working Paper, WP/18/82.

title: Interest Rate-Growth Differential and Debt-Stabilising Primary Balance — dk_rg
Sample Fiscal Data — dk_sample_data
text: Provides built-sample datasets running examples tests without requiring external data.

title: Sample Fiscal Data — dk_sample_data
code: dk_sample_data(country = c("sample", "high_debt"))

title: Sample Fiscal Data — dk_sample_data
text: country Character. sample dataset return. Options: "sample" (default) provides synthetic 20-year history mid-income country; "high_debt" provides high-debt scenario.

title: Sample Fiscal Data — dk_sample_data
text: list components: years Integer vector years. debt Numeric vector debt--GDP ratios. interest_rate Numeric vector effective interest rates government debt. gdp_growth Numeric vector nominal GDP growth rates. primary_balance Numeric vector primary balance--GDP ratios (positive = surplus).

title: IMF Standardised Stress Tests — dk_stress_test
text: Applies six standardised IMF stress-test scenarios baseline debt projection using debt dynamics equation:

title: IMF Standardised Stress Tests — dk_stress_test
code: dk_stress_test(   debt,   interest_rate,   gdp_growth,   primary_balance,   horizon = 5,   growth_shock = -0.01,   interest_shock = 0.02,   exchange_shock = 0.15,   fx_share = 0,   pb_shock = -0.01,   contingent_shock = 0.1,   calibrate = NULL )

title: IMF Standardised Stress Tests — dk_stress_test
text: debt Numeric scalar. Initial debt--GDP ratio. interest_rate Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Baseline nominal effective interest rate. gdp_growth Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Baseline nominal GDP growth rate. primary_balance Numeric scalar vector length horizon. Baseline primary balance share GDP (positive = surplus). horizon Integer scalar. Projection horizon years. Default 5. growth_shock Numeric scalar. Percentage-point reduction GDP growth applied first two years. Default -0.01 (1 pp lower growth). interest_shock Numeric scalar. Percentage-point increase interest rate. Default 0.02 (200 basis points). exchange_shock Numeric scalar. Depreciation fraction applied foreign-currency debt. Default 0.15 (15 per cent depreciation). fx_share Numeric scalar. Share debt denominated foreign currency. Default 0. pb_shock Numeric scalar. Percentage-point deterioration primary balance first two years. Default -0.01. contingent_shock Numeric scalar. One-increase debt--GDP contingent liabilities materialising. Default 0.10. calibrate Optional named list data-driven shock calibration. contain numeric vectors gdp_growth_hist, interest_rate_hist, primary_balance_hist. provided, shock sizes computed one standard deviation historical series, replacing fixed defaults. NULL (default), fixed defaults used.

title: IMF Standardised Stress Tests — dk_stress_test
text: S3 object class dk_stress containing: scenarios data.frame columns year, baseline, growth, interest_rate, exchange_rate, primary_balance, combined, contingent. terminal Named numeric vector terminal debt--GDP scenario. inputs list storing input parameters.

title: IMF Standardised Stress Tests — dk_stress_test
text: $$d_{t+1} = \frac{1 + r_t}{1 + g_t} d_t - pb_t + sfa_t$$ six scenarios : Growth shock: GDP growth reduced growth_shock first two years. Interest rate shock: interest rate increased interest_shock full horizon. Exchange rate shock: debt increases debt * fx_share * exchange_shock year 1 (one-stock-flow adjustment currency depreciation). Primary balance shock: primary balance reduced pb_shock first two years. Combined shock: simultaneous growth shock growth_shock / 2 interest rate shock interest_shock / 2. Contingent liabilities: one-debt increase contingent_shock year 1.

title: IMF Standardised Stress Tests — dk_stress_test
text: International Monetary Fund (2013). Staff Guidance Note Public Debt Sustainability Analysis Market-Access Countries. IMF Policy Paper. International Monetary Fund (2022). Staff Guidance Note Sovereign Risk Debt Sustainability Framework Market Access Countries. IMF Policy Paper.

title: IMF Standardised Stress Tests — dk_stress_test
code: st <- dk_stress_test(   debt = 0.90,   interest_rate = 0.03,   gdp_growth = 0.04,   primary_balance = 0.01,   fx_share = 0.20 ) st #>  #> ── IMF Stress Test Scenarios ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Horizon: 5 years #> • Initial debt/GDP: 90% #>  #> Terminal debt/GDP by scenario: #>  #>  Scenario               Terminal Diff   #>  Baseline               80.9%    0 pp   #>  Growth shock           82.5%    1.7 pp #>  Interest rate shock    89.3%    8.5 pp #>  Exchange rate shock    83.4%    2.6 pp #>  Primary balance shock  82.8%    1.9 pp #>  Combined shock         85.9%    5 pp   #>  Contingent liabilities 90.5%    9.6 pp plot(st)

what: S1 and S2 Sustainability Gap Indicators — dk_sustainability_gap
title: S1 and S2 Sustainability Gap Indicators — dk_sustainability_gap
text: Computes S1 S2 fiscal sustainability gap indicators used European Commission assess size permanent budgetary adjustment required ensure debt sustainability.

title: S1 and S2 Sustainability Gap Indicators — dk_sustainability_gap
code: dk_sustainability_gap(   debt,   structural_balance,   gdp_growth,   interest_rate,   ageing_costs = 0,   target_debt = 0.6,   target_year = 20,   indicator = c("both", "S1", "S2") )

title: S1 and S2 Sustainability Gap Indicators — dk_sustainability_gap
text: debt Numeric scalar. Current debt--GDP ratio. structural_balance Numeric scalar. Current structural primary balance share GDP (positive = surplus). gdp_growth Numeric scalar. Real GDP growth rate. interest_rate Numeric scalar. Real interest rate. ageing_costs Numeric scalar. Projected increase age-related expenditure percentage points GDP. Default 0. target_debt Numeric scalar. Target debt--GDP ratio S1. Default 0.60. target_year Integer scalar. Number years reach target debt ratio. Default 20. indicator Character. indicator compute: "S1", "S2", "" (default).

title: S1 and S2 Sustainability Gap Indicators — dk_sustainability_gap
text: S3 object class dk_sgap containing: S1 S1 sustainability gap (NA requested). S2 S2 sustainability gap (NA requested). risk_S1 Risk classification S1: "low", "medium", "high". risk_S2 Risk classification S2: "low", "medium", "high". required_pb required structural primary balance implied S1. current_pb current structural primary balance. inputs list storing input parameters.

title: S1 and S2 Sustainability Gap Indicators — dk_sustainability_gap
text: S1 measures permanent adjustment structural primary balance needed bring debt--GDP ratio target_debt target_year years, taking account projected increases age-related expenditure. S2 measures permanent adjustment needed stabilise debt--GDP ratio infinite horizon, incorporating full net present value future increases age-related spending.

title: S1 and S2 Sustainability Gap Indicators — dk_sustainability_gap
text: European Commission (2012). Fiscal Sustainability Report 2012. European Economy 8/2012, Directorate-General Economic Financial Affairs.

title: S1 and S2 Sustainability Gap Indicators — dk_sustainability_gap
code: dk_sustainability_gap(   debt = 0.90,   structural_balance = -0.01,   gdp_growth = 0.015,   interest_rate = 0.025,   ageing_costs = 0.02 ) #>  #> ── Sustainability Gap Indicators ─────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Current debt/GDP: 90% #> • Current structural PB: -1% #> • Interest rate: 2.5% #> • GDP growth: 1.5% #>  #>  #> ── S1 Indicator ── #>  #> • Required PB adjustment: 4.3 pp #> • Required structural PB: 2.3% #> • Target debt/GDP: 60% in 20 years #>   Risk: MEDIUM #>  #>  #> ── S2 Indicator ── #>  #> • Required PB adjustment: 204.9 pp #> • Ageing costs: 2 pp #>   Risk: HIGH

title: debtkit 0.1.3
text: dk_gfn() now returns S3 object class dk_gfn dedicated print() plot() methods, consistent debtkit functions.

title: debtkit 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-31 Removed .GlobalEnv modification dk_fan_chart() seed handling, per CRAN policy.

title: debtkit 0.1.1
text: Fixed Bohn (1998) DOI: replaced defunct JSTOR DOI QJE publisher DOI (10.1162/003355398555793).

title: debtkit 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Deterministic debt projections via dk_project() using standard debt dynamics equation. Historical decomposition debt changes interest, growth, primary balance, stock-flow adjustment effects via dk_decompose(). Interest rate-growth differential debt-stabilising primary balance via dk_rg(). Bohn (1998) fiscal reaction function estimation OLS, rolling-window, quadratic methods via dk_bohn_test(). HAC (Newey-West) standard errors via robust_se = TRUE. Stochastic debt fan charts via Monte Carlo simulation using dk_fan_chart() dk_estimate_shocks(). Supports bootstrap residual resampling. Six standardised IMF stress tests via dk_stress_test(), optional data-driven calibration. IMF-style heat map risk assessment via dk_heat_map(). Gross financing needs projection via dk_gfn(). European Commission S1/S2 sustainability gap indicators via dk_sustainability_gap(). Scenario comparison via dk_compare(). Built-sample data via dk_sample_data().
